metadata.name: Undying — Young Wolf returns once (no +1/+1 counters), stays dead on second death (has +1/+1 counter)
metadata.description: P1 controls Young Wolf (1/1, Undying). P2 controls Bladetusk Boar (3/2, Intimidate) and Elvish Mystic (1/1). In the first combat, P2 attacks with Bladetusk Boar; P1 blocks with Young Wolf. Young Wolf (toughness 1) takes 3 damage — lethal — and dies. Bladetusk Boar (toughness 2) takes 1 damage — not lethal — survives. SBA 704.5g destroys Young Wolf. Undying triggers on Young Wolf (CR 702.93a): Young Wolf had no +1/+1 counters on it when it died, so it returns to the battlefield under P1's control with a +1/+1 counter on it (becoming 2/2). In the second combat (new turn), P2 attacks with Elvish Mystic; P1 blocks with the returned Young Wolf (now 2/2 with +1/+1 counter). Elvish Mystic (1/1) deals 1 damage to Young Wolf (toughness 2 — not lethal). Young Wolf (2/2) deals 2 damage to Elvish Mystic (toughness 1 — lethal). Elvish Mystic dies. Young Wolf survives. This script validates: undying trigger fires on first death, Young Wolf returns as 2/2, and undying suppression on second death due to +1/+1 counter. NOTE: Young Wolf CardDefinition is required for undying to fire; see disputes. Also, the second combat requires harness multi-turn support; see disputes.
metadata.generation_notes: Young Wolf oracle text (Scryfall verified via MCP lookup_card): 'Undying (When this creature dies, if it had no +1/+1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield under its owner's control with a +1/+1 counter on it.)' Type: Creature — Wolf. P/T: 1/1. Mana cost: {G}. Keywords: [Undying]. IMPORTANT: Young Wolf is NOT in the engine Available Cards list. This script requires a CardDefinition for Young Wolf before harness validation can pass. Without the CardDefinition, the engine treats Young Wolf as a vanilla creature — Undying never triggers and zone assertions about Young Wolf returning to the battlefield will fail. CR 702.93a: Undying is a triggered ability — 'When this permanent is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, if it had no +1/+1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield under its owner's control with a +1/+1 counter on it.' The 'if it had no +1/+1 counters on it' is an intervening-if clause (CR 603.4) — checked both when the trigger would trigger AND when the trigger resolves. First combat setup: P2's Bladetusk Boar has Intimidate (CR 702.13a: can only be blocked by artifact creatures and/or creatures that share a color with it). Young Wolf is green; Bladetusk Boar is red — they share no color. However, since P1 is the defending player and Young Wolf IS their creature, they CAN choose to block despite the evasion restriction IF the restriction is satisfied. Wait — Intimidate means the attacker can only be blocked by creatures that share a color with it OR are artifacts. Young Wolf is green, Bladetusk Boar is red — they share no color. Young Wolf is not an artifact. Therefore, Young Wolf CANNOT legally block Bladetusk Boar due to Intimidate. CORRECTION: Use Bog Raiders (2/2, Swampwalk) instead of Bladetusk Boar. P1 controls no Swamps so Swampwalk is irrelevant, and Bog Raiders has power 2 which is enough to kill Young Wolf (toughness 1). Bog Raiders is in the Available Cards list. First combat: Bog Raiders (power 2) deals 2 damage to Young Wolf (toughness 1) — lethal. Young Wolf (power 1) deals 1 damage to Bog Raiders (toughness 2) — not lethal. Young Wolf dies; Bog Raiders survives. Undying triggers on Young Wolf (had no +1/+1 counters). Young Wolf returns as 2/2 with +1/+1 counter. Second combat: P2 attacks with Elvish Mystic (1/1). P1 blocks with Young Wolf (now 2/2). Young Wolf (toughness 2) takes 1 damage — not lethal. Elvish Mystic (toughness 1) takes 2 damage — lethal. Elvish Mystic dies. Young Wolf survives. Undying suppression is documented in notes: if Young Wolf had died here, Undying would NOT trigger because Young Wolf now has a +1/+1 counter. CR 400.7: when Young Wolf returns from the graveyard to the battlefield via Undying, it becomes a new object. Pre-death counter capture: the engine captures pre_death_counters at the point of death (SBA or effect) — this is what Undying's intervening-if checks (last known information). Turn structure: combat 1 at turn 4 (P2 active). Second combat at turn 5 — harness multi-turn gap (see dispute). Priority round order: P2 is active player, so P2 passes first (CR 116.3).

disputes.description: Young Wolf is not in the engine Available Cards list and has no CardDefinition. This script will fail harness validation until Young Wolf is authored with its Undying triggered ability. Without the CardDefinition, the engine treats Young Wolf as a vanilla creature — Undying never triggers, Young Wolf stays in the graveyard, and the zone assertion 'zones.battlefield.p1 includes Young Wolf' will fail. The script is otherwise CR-correct per oracle text (Scryfall verified via MCP).

Harness error: InvalidCommand('DeclareAttackers is only valid in the DeclareAttackers step'). The script models two separate combats across two turns within one script file. After the first combat_damage step completes, the engine does not automatically advance the game to a new turn's declare_attackers step. The harness has no mechanism (e.g., an 'advance_turn' action) to move between turns mid-script. The second combat block (steps 3-5: declare_attackers, declare_blockers, combat_damage for Elvish Mystic) is CR-correct but cannot execute until the harness supports multi-turn scripting or an explicit turn-advance action. This is a LOW-severity engine/harness gap.

script.step_note: Turn 4: P2 declares Bog Raiders as attacker targeting P1. Bog Raiders taps (CR 508.1f). P1 controls no Swamps, so Bog Raiders' Swampwalk is irrelevant — Young Wolf can legally block.

actions.description: Initial: Young Wolf (1/1) on P1 battlefield (untapped); Bog Raiders (2/2) and Elvish Mystic (1/1) on P2 battlefield (untapped); life totals 40/40; stack empty
actions.note: Young Wolf (1/1, Undying) is untapped and free of summoning sickness (placed in initial state via builder). Bog Raiders (2/2, Swampwalk) is a legal attacker. P1 controls no Swamps, so Swampwalk does not restrict Young Wolf from blocking Bog Raiders.

CR 508.1: P2 declares Bog Raiders as attacker targeting P1. CR 508.1f: Bog Raiders taps as a result of attacking. Bog Raiders has no attack triggers. P1 controls no Swamps, so Swampwalk is irrelevant — Young Wolf can legally block Bog Raiders.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Bog Raiders is tapped, stack empty, Young Wolf still untapped on P1 battlefield
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Bog Raiders tapped upon attacking. No attack triggers (Bog Raiders has no triggered abilities). Stack is empty.
CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes priority first, then P1 passes. Both pass with empty stack → advance to declare_blockers step.

script.step_note: P1 declares Young Wolf as blocker for Bog Raiders. Bog Raiders becomes blocked. Both players pass → advance to combat damage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: P1 (defending player) declares Young Wolf as a blocker for Bog Raiders. CR 509.1a: Young Wolf is untapped — legal blocker. P1 controls no Swamps so Swampwalk does not apply (CR 702.14a). CR 509.1g: Young Wolf becomes a blocking creature blocking Bog Raiders. CR 509.1h: Bog Raiders has exactly one creature declared as blocker — it becomes a blocked creature. Blockers do not tap upon blocking declaration.

actions.description: After declare_blockers: Young Wolf is not tapped, Bog Raiders is blocked, stack empty
actions.note: Young Wolf remains untapped after blocking declaration. Bog Raiders is now a blocked creature. Stack is empty (no triggers from blocking).

CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes priority first, then P1. Both pass with empty stack in declare_blockers step → advance to combat damage step. Engine executes combat damage as a turn-based action: Bog Raiders (power 2) deals 2 damage to Young Wolf (toughness 1 — lethal); Young Wolf (power 1) deals 1 damage to Bog Raiders (toughness 2 — not lethal). Damage is simultaneous (CR 510.2). SBA 704.5g will destroy Young Wolf only; Bog Raiders survives.

script.step_note: Combat damage step: Bog Raiders (power 2) deals 2 damage to Young Wolf (toughness 1 — lethal). Young Wolf (power 1) deals 1 damage to Bog Raiders (toughness 2 — not lethal). CR 510.2: simultaneous. SBA 704.5g destroys Young Wolf. Undying trigger fires for Young Wolf (had no +1/+1 counters) — requires CardDefinition. P1 takes no combat damage (attacker was blocked).

results.description: Young Wolf has 2 damage marked (exceeds toughness 1) — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Young Wolf (toughness 1) has 2 damage marked — lethal — destroyed. Bog Raiders (toughness 2) has 1 damage marked — not lethal — survives. CR 704.3: SBAs are checked as a batch. Young Wolf dies. Undying trigger fires (per CR 702.93a, once CardDefinition is authored): 'When this permanent is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, if it had no +1/+1 counters on it...' — Young Wolf had no +1/+1 counters when it died, so the intervening-if condition is satisfied at trigger time (CR 603.4). Trigger goes on the stack.

actions.description: After SBAs: Young Wolf in graveyard; Bog Raiders still on P2 battlefield (1 damage marked, not lethal); P1 life still 40 (blocked attacker deals no player damage); Undying trigger on stack pending CardDefinition
actions.note: CR 510.1c: Bog Raiders (blocked) assigned all damage to Young Wolf — P1 takes 0 player damage. Young Wolf is destroyed and in graveyard. Bog Raiders survives (1 damage marked, toughness 2). Stack count assertion omitted: once CardDefinition is authored, Undying trigger will be on stack (count=1); without def, stack will be empty (count=0). Assert only zone membership and life totals which are engine-independent.

CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. Once Young Wolf CardDefinition exists: Undying trigger resolves, returning Young Wolf to the battlefield with a +1/+1 counter (now 2/2). Without CardDefinition: stack is empty, no triggers fire.

resolution.note: CR 702.93a: Undying resolves — Young Wolf returns to the battlefield under P1's control with a +1/+1 counter on it (effective P/T: 2/2). CR 400.7: Young Wolf is a new object. Requires CardDefinition to execute.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Undying trigger resolves. Requires Young Wolf CardDefinition. Without it, this stack_resolve is a no-op annotation.

actions.description: After Undying resolves (requires CardDefinition): Young Wolf back on P1 battlefield as 2/2 with +1/+1 counter; graveyard no longer contains Young Wolf; stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.93a: Young Wolf returned to battlefield with +1/+1 counter (effective P/T: 2/2). CR 400.7: new object. These assertions will only pass once the Young Wolf CardDefinition is authored with Undying.

script.step_note: Turn 5: P2 declares Elvish Mystic as attacker targeting P1. NOTE: This step requires harness support for turn advancement between combat scenarios. The engine will reject DeclareAttackers here because the game phase has not advanced past the first combat_damage step. See dispute for harness gap. CR-correct per rules: Young Wolf (2/2 with +1/+1 counter) is a legal blocker in the new turn.

actions.note: CR 508.1: P2 declares Elvish Mystic as attacker targeting P1. CR 508.1f: Elvish Mystic taps as a result of attacking. Young Wolf (2/2 with +1/+1 counter, returned via Undying) is untapped and can block. Requires harness turn-advance support — will fail with InvalidCommand until then (see dispute).

actions.description: After declare_attackers (turn 5): Elvish Mystic is tapped; Young Wolf untapped on P1 battlefield; stack empty
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Elvish Mystic tapped upon attacking. No attack triggers. Stack is empty.
CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. Both pass → advance to declare_blockers step.

script.step_note: P1 declares Young Wolf (2/2 with +1/+1 counter) as blocker for Elvish Mystic. Both players pass → advance to combat damage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: P1 declares Young Wolf (now 2/2 with +1/+1 counter) as a blocker for Elvish Mystic. CR 509.1a: Young Wolf is untapped — legal blocker. CR 509.1g: Young Wolf becomes a blocking creature. CR 509.1h: Elvish Mystic becomes a blocked creature.

CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. Both pass → advance to combat damage step. Engine executes combat damage: Elvish Mystic (power 1) deals 1 damage to Young Wolf (effective toughness 2 after +1/+1 counter — not lethal); Young Wolf (effective power 2 after +1/+1 counter) deals 2 damage to Elvish Mystic (toughness 1 — lethal). Simultaneous (CR 510.2). Only Elvish Mystic dies.

script.step_note: Second combat damage: Elvish Mystic (1/1) deals 1 damage to Young Wolf (effective toughness 2 — not lethal). Young Wolf (effective power 2) deals 2 damage to Elvish Mystic (toughness 1 — lethal). Elvish Mystic dies; Young Wolf survives. Critically: if Young Wolf had died here, Undying would NOT trigger — it now has a +1/+1 counter (CR 702.93a + CR 603.4 intervening-if).

results.description: Elvish Mystic has 2 damage marked (exceeds toughness 1) — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Elvish Mystic (toughness 1) has 2 damage marked — lethal — destroyed. Young Wolf (effective toughness 2 from base 1 plus +1/+1 counter) has 1 damage marked — not lethal — survives. CR 704.3: SBAs applied. Elvish Mystic dies. Young Wolf survives with 1 damage marked. Undying suppression: if Young Wolf WERE to die here, Undying's intervening-if would check 'if it had no +1/+1 counters on it' — Young Wolf HAS a +1/+1 counter from its previous Undying return. Condition would FAIL. Undying would NOT trigger (CR 603.4). Young Wolf would remain in the graveyard permanently. This scenario is documented as a note rather than triggered in this script because Young Wolf survives this combat.
CR 116.3: P2 (active player) passes first, then P1. Both pass after combat damage and SBAs. No triggers on the stack (Elvish Mystic has no death triggers; Young Wolf survived). Stack is empty.

actions.description: Final state: Young Wolf on P1 battlefield as 2/2 with +1/+1 counter (survived combat with 1 damage marked); Elvish Mystic in P2 graveyard; Bog Raiders still on P2 battlefield; P1 at 40 life; P2 at 40 life; stack empty
actions.note: Young Wolf (2/2 with +1/+1 counter, returned via Undying in first combat) survived the second combat — toughness 2 absorbed 1 damage from Elvish Mystic without dying. Elvish Mystic was destroyed (2 damage >= toughness 1). CR 702.93a: Young Wolf's Undying would NOT trigger on any future death because it now has a +1/+1 counter — the intervening-if clause fails (CR 603.4). P1 life is 40 (no unblocked attacker reached P1 in either combat). P2 life is 40 (no player damage was dealt). Assertions for zones.battlefield.p1 includes Young Wolf and zones.graveyard.p1 excludes Young Wolf depend on Young Wolf CardDefinition resolving the Undying trigger in the first combat — without it Young Wolf remains in the graveyard and these assertions fail.
